intake.risk_posture: Preserve existing /builder behavior strictly; allow new visible UX only on isolated /builder-v2 with explicit product/design stop points.
intake.harness_policy: Product docs and goal packet updates allowed; no AGENTS.md, lint, dependency, or plugin changes without a routed maintenance task and explicit approval.
intake.stop_condition: Stop when the active tranche lands and is verified, a product/design decision is needed, verification fails twice on the same blocker, same-concept expansion crosses protected scope, or the work starts drifting into rushed public-shipping increments rather than healthy local groundwork.

focus_areas.name: New route and feature boundary
focus_areas.evidence: W1 added a lazy BuilderV2Page route in src/App.tsx and kept Builder V2 absent from NAV_ITEMS.
focus_areas.next: Keep /builder-v2 unpromoted until a later product/design decision.

focus_areas.name: Current builder contract reuse
focus_areas.evidence: W1 added useBuilderV2Model as a facade over builderDraftStore, persistence, ownership hydration, search helpers, and team-state assignment/removal helpers; W2 extended it to gear/posse assignment; W3 wired quick-lineup; W4 wired mobile drawer orchestration; W6 wired import/export; W7 wired transfer confirmation; W8 wired team management.
focus_areas.next: Terminal for this goal. DnD and recommendations are future UX-mechanics slices after the designer pass.

focus_areas.name: Old UX branch flow salvage
focus_areas.evidence: W4/R4 implemented the concept-image mobile overview -> focused builder -> full-screen picker drawer flow on /builder-v2 while keeping the old builder/mobile-ux branch as reference only.
focus_areas.next: Keep old branch evidence available only for future tablet/team polish; do not migrate old branch code into Builder V2.

focus_areas.name: Test and characterization harness
focus_areas.evidence: W1-W8 added focused V2 model/page characterization for assignments, gear/posse, quick-lineup, mobile drawer focus, adaptive routing, import/export, transfer confirmations, and team management while keeping relevant V1 contract tests green.
focus_areas.next: Terminal for this goal. Add focused characterization with any future UX-mechanics slice.

focus_areas.name: Visual baseline
focus_areas.evidence: W1 added local builder-v2 CSS using sharp panels, compact controls, restrained blue, amber selection state, and D-Zone-style UI tokens. C9 reframes this as product baseline, not final visual destination.
focus_areas.next: Terminal for this goal. The designer pass owns final visual polish toward the selected mockups.

focus_areas.name: Concept image alignment
focus_areas.evidence: W1 follows Desktop.png anatomy; W3 implemented the quick-lineup functional contract; W4 implemented Mobile.png-style overview/focused/full-screen picker drawer states; W5 added the pseudo-desktop adaptive shell; C9 names the selected concept images as the designer target.
focus_areas.next: Terminal for this goal. Final visual interpretation belongs to the designer pass.

focus_areas.name: Teams section polish
focus_areas.evidence: W8/R8 implemented a visually provisional but functional Builder V2 team management overview across desktop, adaptive, and mobile: add, switch, rename, reset/delete confirmations, D-Tide templates, and simple accessible reorder now use existing V1 team helper contracts. C9 records final list/cards/share shape as future design/product work.
focus_areas.next: Terminal for this goal. Final cards/list/share presentation is a designer/future-day decision, not a C9 blocker.

focus_areas.name: DnD/import/export/quick-lineup polish
focus_areas.evidence: W3/R3 implemented quick-lineup, W6/R6 implemented import/export parity, and W7/R7 implemented transfer dialog parity. User clarified DnD and recommendations should wait until the visual/layout direction is good.
focus_areas.next: Terminal for this goal. DnD and recommendations are future UX-mechanics slices after the designer pass, not blockers for C9.

focus_areas.name: Designer handoff readiness
focus_areas.evidence: C9 added docs/refactor-goals/builder-v2-rebuild/designer-handoff.md, current baseline screenshots in artifacts/, concept-image target framing, and a pasteable designer prompt that treats /builder-v2 as product baseline rather than visual destination.

candidates.title: Builder V2 route and bare draft loop shell

candidates.title: Minimal V2 picker surface
candidates.evidence: W1 added BuilderV2AwakenerPicker with current search/sort helpers and disabled future category tabs.

candidates.title: Concept-image-informed V2 shell anatomy
candidates.evidence: W1 implemented the desktop rail/stage/armory/overview shell and mobile single-column collapse, with quick-lineup/drawer polish explicitly kept for later.

candidates.title: Gear and posse assignment
candidates.evidence: W2 added Builder V2 Armory category tabs, actionable W1/W2/Covenant and team Posse targets, click-to-assign/clear flows, same-active-team gear movement, duplicate guards, and focused tests.
candidates.next: Terminal for this goal. Transfer dialogs are implemented; DnD/recommendations are future UX-mechanics slices.

candidates.title: Mobile overview/focused/picker drawer flow
candidates.evidence: W4/R4 added a mobile-only Builder V2 layout with overview, focused slot builder, shared picker drawer content, dialog semantics, Escape/search focus/focus return, mobile quick-lineup step focus synchronization, focused tests, and browser smoke.

candidates.title: Quick Lineup V2 mode
candidates.evidence: W3 enabled Builder V2 Quick Lineup mode using existing builderDraftStore quick-lineup actions, compact V2 controls, assignment-driven step advance, manual target jump, cancel restore, final posse completion, and focused model/page tests.

candidates.title: Tablet/adaptive workbench shell
concept_blast_radius: focused viewport tests and browser smoke
candidates.evidence: W5/R5 implemented and reviewed a distinct adaptive Builder V2 shell for 769px..66rem: BuilderV2Page now routes mobile/adaptive/desktop by viewport, adaptive widths render a compact teams rail plus active workbench, and the picker opens in a modal drawer with backdrop, body scroll lock, search focus, Escape focus return, hidden background semantics, and in-drawer validation alerts.

candidates.title: Team overview readiness component
candidates.evidence: W8/R8 added a shared BuilderV2TeamManagement surface used by desktop, adaptive, and mobile layouts, with screenshot-friendly team summaries including slots, posse, support, wheel, and covenant tags.
candidates.next: Terminal for this goal. Final share/card/list polish is future design/product work after the designer pass.

candidates.title: Builder V2 import/export parity
concept_blast_radius: existing import/export codecs and planner reuse
candidates.evidence: W6/R6 reused the existing import/export codecs, planner, validation, and shared dialogs from V2: desktop/adaptive/mobile now expose provisional import/export actions, t1 import into empty active team, mt1 replace, duplicate override, conflict strategy, active/all standard export, and active in-game export are covered by focused V2 tests.

candidates.title: Builder V2 transfer dialog parity
candidates.evidence: W7/R7 reused existing transfer request, dialog, and resolution semantics in Builder V2: cross-team awakeners, wheels, and posses now open confirmation dialogs; cancel is non-mutating; confirm moves clear the source owner/socket; Use as Support keeps source ownership, marks the target support, sets level 90; adaptive/mobile drawer handoff and quick-lineup wait-for-confirm behavior are covered.
candidates.next: Terminal for this goal. DnD transfer polish is future UX-mechanics work.

candidates.title: Teams overview redesign
candidates.evidence: W8/R8 implemented the functional C7 MVP using existing team-collection and builderDraftStore contracts: add/max, switch, inline rename commit/cancel/blank no-op, empty delete/reset direct behavior, non-empty delete/reset confirmations, D-Tide 5/10 template confirmation, and up/down reorder preserving active identity.
candidates.next: Terminal for this goal. Final list/cards/share presentation belongs to designer/future polish.

candidates.title: Import/export and transfer parity
candidates.evidence: W6/R6 implemented Builder V2 import/export parity and W7/R7 implemented Builder V2 transfer dialog parity, closing the actionable C8 split without editing protected V1 builder code.
candidates.next: Terminal for this goal. DnD/recommendations remain future UX-mechanics slices rather than C8 blockers.

candidates.title: Designer handoff bundle
candidates.evidence: C9 added designer-handoff.md, Browser-plugin desktop captures, local mobile/adaptive viewport captures, concept-image traceability, explicit visual/layout scope, deferred UX-mechanic notes, and a pasteable designer prompt naming the mockups as the end goal.
candidates.next: Terminal for this goal. Use the handoff as the next prompt source for the designer/design model.

maintenance.title: Builder V2 contract docs
maintenance.next: Superseded by the Builder V2 goal packet plus designer-handoff.md for this local rebuild. Add dedicated contract docs only if Builder V2 is promoted later.

maintenance.title: Builder visual vocabulary drift guard
maintenance.next: No lint-law or AGENTS drift guard was required in this goal; visual vocabulary guidance is captured in designer-handoff.md and existing DESIGN.md/PRODUCT.md.
evidence: No repeated visual-vocabulary drift guard or AGENTS/lint update was required during W1-W9; user scoped C9 to designer handoff rather than harness changes.

tasks.title: Map Builder V2 rebuild scope
tasks.expected_output: Evidence map for contracts, UX flow, tests, visual anchors, route integration, and interaction risks.

receipt.summary: Six read-only scouts mapped Builder V2 contracts, old branch UX flow, test coverage, visual anchors, route integration, and core interaction risks.
evidence: Current contracts: reuse builderDraftStore, public-id migrations/persistence, team-state helpers, ownership projection, action factories, import/export helpers, DnD ids, and detail modal store.
Old branch: keep mobile overview/focused/picker/quick-lineup and tablet/desktop flow as reference only; drop old v2 store/persistence/action integration.
Tests: pure helper/store tests carry; current page tests are DOM-coupled and should be translated into V2 outcome tests.
Design: anchor to DESIGN/PRODUCT tokens, D-Zone/Timeline/database patterns, and docs/design screenshots; avoid old builder blue vocabulary.
Routing: add lazy /builder-v2 route in src/App.tsx, avoid nav entry in first slice.
Interaction risks: avoid direct slot mutation; keep helper paths for realm caps, duplicate identity, support transfer, quick-lineup focus, and overlay details.

tasks.title: Approve first Builder V2 Worker slice
tasks.expected_output: One bounded Worker concept scope for C1/C2/C3 with invariants, validation, rollback, and stop conditions.

receipt.summary: Approved first Worker as C1 plus smallest viable C2/C3: an isolated /builder-v2 route and bare awakener draft loop shell.
receipt.concept: Builder V2 route and bare awakener draft loop shell
allowed_files: src/features/builder-v2/*.css only if Tailwind classes become unreadable
invariants: /builder remains protected and behaviorally untouched.
/builder-v2 is lazy-routed but absent from navigation.
V2 uses public ids and existing builderDraftStore; no new persistence/store/migration contract.
Slot assignment/removal goes through current helper/model paths.
Concept images guide layout density and responsive shape without being imported into runtime UI.

tasks.title: Map concept image cues for first Builder V2 shell
tasks.expected_output: First-slice visual/layout cues and deferrals from concept images.

receipt.summary: Visual scout mapped non-negotiable first-slice cues from the concept images.
evidence: Desktop shell should read as left team rail, center active builder, right picker/armory, with team overview below.
Active team area should expose shallow team/posse header, quick lineup placeholder, four slot cards, and selected/editing state.
Mobile concepts should guide future app-like flow, not force full mobile drawer and quick-lineup implementation into the first slice.
Amber should remain state/current selection rather than decorative glow.

tasks.title: Build isolated Builder V2 awakener draft loop shell
tasks.expected_output: Lazy /builder-v2 route, Builder V2 feature folder, thin model facade, concept-informed shell anatomy, four-slot active team UI, minimal awakener picker, assign/remove behavior, and focused tests.

receipt.slice: Builder V2 route and bare awakener draft loop shell
receipt.what_got_simpler: Builder V2 now has an isolated route, feature folder, and typed model facade so the shell can render and mutate draft slot state without importing the current BuilderPage UI.
receipt.behavior_preserved: /builder remained untouched; V2 assignment/removal goes through current team-state helpers, current draft store, current persistence loader/saver, current search helpers, and current identity duplicate handling.
receipt.current_complexity: Current production builder remains unchanged and debt-heavy; V2 first slice is one route component, one model hook, two presentational surfaces, local CSS, and focused tests.
receipt.expected_complexity_after: Later slices can add gear, posse, mobile drawer, teams, and import/export by extending the facade/components instead of editing the current monolith.
receipt.root_fix_patch_decision: Root-fix slice: create a new Builder V2 boundary instead of patching the current BuilderPage.
receipt.dependency_review: No dependency changes made or needed.
receipt.harness_update: No AGENTS.md, lint, or plugin harness changes made; goal packet updated only.
validation: npx vitest run src/features/builder-v2/useBuilderV2Model.test.ts src/features/builder-v2/BuilderV2Page.test.tsx (2 files, 10 tests passed)
npx vitest run src/features/builder/useBuilderViewModel.test.ts src/features/builder/builder-persistence.test.ts src/features/builder/builder-ownership-projection.test.ts (3 files, 50 tests passed)
npm run test:integration (7 files, 57 tests passed)
Playwright smoke: http://127.0.0.1:5173/#/builder-v2 desktop 1440x1000 and mobile 390x844 rendered; console output was only React DevTools info
risks: V2 currently shares the existing builder persistence key by design, so visiting /builder-v2 can hydrate/save the same local draft as /builder.
Mobile is a responsive stack only; drawer, app-like focused flows, and quick-lineup are still queued.
Teams overview remains intentionally minimal pending list-vs-cards product direction.
learning_lint_candidates: Consider a Builder V2 contract note after review if the facade boundary holds through the next tranche.

tasks.title: Review W1 Builder V2 route and draft loop shell
tasks.expected_output: Review the W1 diff, receipts, validation, and residual risk; decide whether the tranche needs fixes or can queue the next Builder V2 slice.

receipt.summary: Review found one blocking data-integrity issue, fixed in W1 before tranche close.
findings: V2 initially assigned awakeners through active-team helpers only, so an awakener already used by another non-support team could be duplicated across teams and autosaved into shared builder persistence.
fix: useBuilderV2Model now builds a non-support usedAwakenerByIdentityKey map like V1, respects the current allow-duplicates preference key, and blocks cross-team assignments when V2 has no transfer dialog available.
useBuilderV2Model.test.ts added a regression test proving an awakener already assigned to Team 2 cannot be assigned into Team 1 from V2.
validation: npx vitest run src/features/builder-v2/useBuilderV2Model.test.ts src/features/builder-v2/BuilderV2Page.test.tsx (2 files, 10 tests passed)
npx vitest run src/features/builder/useBuilderViewModel.test.ts src/features/builder/builder-persistence.test.ts src/features/builder/builder-ownership-projection.test.ts (3 files, 50 tests passed)
npm run test:integration (7 files, 57 tests passed)
residual_risk: Full transfer dialog parity remains queued for a later V2 slice.
Untracked screenshot/mockup artifacts remain intentionally outside the product commit unless selected later.

receipt.summary: Three read-only scouts mapped C4 contracts, pinned tests, and concept-image-aligned UI shape.
evidence: TeamSlot already carries wheels and covenantId, while Team carries posseId.
Existing pure helpers cover wheel and covenant assign, clear, and swap behavior in team-state.ts.
Posse is team-level state handled through active team updates; V1 transfer behavior remains a larger deferred parity concern.
V1 tests pin same-team wheel movement, cross-team transfer requests, covenant assignment/swap, and posse transfer behavior.
C4 UI should make W1/W2/Covenant strip targets real buttons, make the header Posse target actionable, and activate Armory tabs for Awakeners, Wheels, Covenants, and Posses.
Mobile drawer, DnD, and full transfer dialogs should remain deferred for this slice.

tasks.title: Approve Builder V2 gear and posse Worker slice

in_scope: Add Armory tabs for Awakeners, Wheels, Covenants, and Posses.
Reuse the existing Armory row-list pattern for all categories.

receipt.what_got_simpler: The V2 model facade now owns one coherent click-to-assign path for awakeners, wheels, covenants, and posses instead of leaving gear as inert labels or relying on the old BuilderPage shell.
receipt.behavior_preserved: /builder and shared builder contracts remained untouched; wheel and covenant updates use existing team-state helpers, active-team wheel reuse swaps/moves, cross-team duplicate wheel/posse assignment is blocked until transfer parity exists, and V2 still shares current draft persistence by design.
receipt.current_complexity: Builder V2 now has one model hook, one armory surface with four tabs, one slot card surface with actionable loadout targets, and focused tests for the assignment loop.
receipt.expected_complexity_after: Later slices can add transfer dialogs, DnD, recommendations, quick-lineup, and mobile drawer behavior without first inventing core gear/posse state flow.

tasks.title: Review W2 Builder V2 gear and posse assignment flow
tasks.expected_output: Review W2 diff, receipts, validation, residual risk, and scope boundaries.

receipt.summary: Review found two low-severity no-op repeat-click issues, both fixed before tranche close.
findings: Repeatedly clicking an already assigned wheel on its selected socket showed a false failure message.
Repeatedly clicking an already assigned covenant on its selected slot showed a false failure message.
fix: useBuilderV2Model now treats no-op same-wheel and same-covenant assignment as quiet state preservation.
useBuilderV2Model.test.ts added a regression test for repeated wheel and covenant assignments.

receipt.summary: Three read-only scouts mapped mobile drawer/focused flow, quick-lineup contracts, and queued candidate ordering after W2.
evidence: Mobile.png defines C5 as three mobile states: Team Overview, Active Character Builder, and Picker as a full-screen drawer, not the current stacked armory.
builder/mobile-ux modeled overview/focused/picker/quick-lineup flow, but should remain flow evidence only because its store/persistence and drawer semantics are outdated.
quick-lineup.ts and builderDraftStore already expose reusable quick-lineup session, focus, advance, back, cancel, finish, and jump behavior.
V2 has assignment actions for awakeners, wheels, covenants, and posses after W2, so C6 is unlocked without touching shared store/helper behavior.
C7 remains product/design blocked by list-vs-cards uncertainty; C8 remains broader parity work around import/export and transfer dialogs.
receipt.recommendation: C6 is structurally safer before C5 because it proves guided focus/picker synchronization before mobile drawer focus management.

tasks.title: Approve Builder V2 Quick Lineup Worker slice
tasks.expected_output: One bounded Worker scope for C6 with in-scope behavior, deferrals, invariants, validation, rollback, stop conditions, and acceptance criteria.

receipt.summary: Approved C6 as the next bounded Builder V2 Worker slice, ahead of C5 mobile drawer work.
receipt.decision: Activate C6: Quick Lineup V2 mode. Do not activate C5 yet.
in_scope: Enable the existing disabled Quick Team Lineup button on /builder-v2.
Expose quickLineupSession and start/advance/back/skip/finish/cancel actions through useBuilderV2Model by consuming existing builderDraftStore actions.
Synchronize quick-lineup focus into V2 pickerTab, activeSelection, and activeTeamTarget, including the posse step.
Add compact visible quick-lineup controls/status in the active team surface.
After successful V2 assignments, advance to the next quick-lineup step using existing store/helper semantics.
Support cancel restoring the original active team state and finish ending quick-lineup without changing /builder.
Keep controls keyboard-accessible with real buttons and stable labels.
deferred: C5 mobile overview/focused/full-screen picker drawer and focus trap.
Transfer dialogs and cross-team move confirmations.
Any shared store/helper behavior changes unless scope expansion is approved.
validation: Browser smoke /builder-v2 at desktop and mobile widths: start quick lineup, assign awakener, advance/back, cancel restores state.

tasks.title: Build Builder V2 Quick Lineup mode
tasks.expected_output: Enable Builder V2 Quick Lineup mode through the V2 model/page/components/tests, using existing builderDraftStore quick-lineup actions and preserving all shared builder behavior.

receipt.what_got_simpler: Builder V2 now exposes a single guided quick-lineup path through the existing V2 model facade instead of keeping the Quick Team Lineup entry point as inert UI.
receipt.behavior_preserved: /builder files and shared builderDraftStore/quick-lineup helpers were not edited; V2 consumes existing quick-lineup store actions and keeps W1/W2 duplicate and assignment guards intact.
receipt.current_complexity: Builder V2 has a model-owned quickLineupSession view, compact page controls, and existing slot/picker surfaces that synchronize through activeSelection/pickerTab.
receipt.expected_complexity_after: C5 mobile drawer/focused layout can consume a proven guided focus contract instead of inventing separate mobile selection semantics.
data_access_render_invariants: Quick-lineup uses public-id Team/TeamSlot contracts from the current builder store.
Starting quick-lineup clears the active team via existing builderDraftStore behavior and cancel restores the original team.
Successful V2 assignment actions advance quick-lineup without bypassing existing team-state helper validation.
During quick-lineup, destructive clear/remove controls are hidden from V2 slot cards to avoid conflicting with guided focus.
receipt.root_fix_patch_decision: Bounded root-fix slice inside the Builder V2 facade/UI boundary; no old BuilderPage edits and no shared store/helper changes.
receipt.dependency_review: No dependency changes made or needed.
receipt.characterization: Added V2 model/page tests for start, assignment advance, back/next, cancel restore, manual target jump, and final posse completion; existing V1 builder suite stayed green.
receipt.harness_update: No AGENTS.md, lint, dependency, or plugin harness changes made; goal packet updated only.

tasks.title: Map C5 mobile layout and drawer test scope
tasks.expected_output: Read-only evidence for the C5 mobile overview/focused/full-screen picker drawer implementation seam and focused failing tests.

receipt.summary: Two read-only scouts mapped C5 as a mobile shell/orchestration slice around the existing W3 quick-lineup and picker contracts.
evidence: Current Builder V2 data flow already exposes pickerTab, activeSelection, activeTeamTarget, quickLineupSession, assignment actions, and quick-lineup controls through useBuilderV2Model.
Current mobile behavior is CSS stacking only; it does not provide concept-image overview/focused/picker app states, dialog semantics, Escape handling, or focus return.

The C5 implementation seam should keep model logic in useBuilderV2Model, keep desktop behavior stable, extract reusable picker content from the current armory aside, and add mobile-only orchestration under src/features/builder-v2/**.
The smallest useful tests should assert mobile picker dialog naming, correct picker tab/search focus for slot and wheel targets, Escape close, and focus return to the invoking control.
Out of scope remains shared store/helper changes, current /builder edits, import/export, DnD, transfer dialogs, recommendations, and deep teams redesign.

behavior_added: Mobile /builder-v2 now branches into an app-like overview, focused slot builder, and full-screen picker drawer at <= 768px.

Mobile slot, wheel, covenant, and posse targets open a named dialog with the correct picker tab and search focus.

Mobile quick-lineup derives the visible focused slot from quickLineupSession.currentStep so assignment advance and Back/Next stay aligned with the guided target.

Builder V2 still uses the existing useBuilderV2Model assignment and quick-lineup store semantics rather than introducing a mobile-specific model.
Untracked docs/design and smoke screenshot artifacts were left unstaged/excluded from W4 packaging.

receipt.dependency_review: No dependency changes made or needed.
characterization: Observed red run before implementation: BuilderV2Page mobile tests failed because overview/dialog/focus behavior did not exist.
Observed red run during browser-found edge case: reopened same mobile target after Escape assigned to the wrong slot until the target selection guard was added.

evidence: Tablet/adaptive shell is a viable standalone slice because JS switches to mobile only at <= 768px while CSS collapses the desktop shell at <= 66rem, leaving 769-1056px as an undesigned stacked desktop fallback.
C8 is viable if split: import/export parity can reuse existing builder import/export helpers/codecs and V2 model seams, while cross-team transfer confirmation should remain a later C8b slice.

tasks.title: Build C8b Builder V2 transfer dialog parity
protected_files: src/features/builder/** except read-only imports/references to existing transfer helpers/components
invariants: /builder behavior remains untouched.
/builder-v2 remains unpromoted from navigation.
Use existing transfer request/dialog/resolution semantics where practical; do not invent a second transfer model.
Cross-team conflicts must not mutate teams before explicit confirmation.
Cancel leaves source and target teams unchanged and preserves the current V2 target/focus state.
Confirm move clears the source owner and assigns the target through current public-id Team state.
Use-as-support, when offered for awakeners, keeps the source owner, marks the target as support, sets level 90, and respects realm/support constraints.
Wheel transfer clears only the source wheel socket and fills the target wheel socket.
Posse transfer clears the source team posse and assigns the active team posse.
Desktop, adaptive, and mobile flows must avoid stacked competing modals; picker drawer/background must not remain interactable behind transfer confirmation.
Quick Lineup advances only after a confirmed successful transfer/support action; cancel stays on the same step.
characterization_first: Model/page red test: assigning an awakener already used by Team 2 from Team 1 opens a Move dialog and does not mutate either team before confirmation.
Page red test: confirming the awakener transfer moves it to the active target slot and clears the source slot.
Page or model red test: Use as Support is available when no support is present, keeps source ownership, assigns target as support, and sets level 90.
Page/model red test: assigning a wheel already used by another team opens a Move dialog; confirm clears source wheel and assigns target wheel.
Page/model red test: assigning a posse already used by another team opens a Move dialog; confirm clears source posse and assigns active team posse.
Adaptive/mobile red test: transfer triggered from picker drawer produces one accessible confirmation modal, no interactable hidden picker/background, and predictable focus after cancel/confirm.
Quick Lineup red test: cross-team transfer during a guided step waits for confirmation before advancing; cancel does not advance.
tasks.rollback: Delete/revert C8b-local V2 edits and return to W6 behavior where cross-team conflicts are blocked with messages.
scope_expansion_rules: If Worker needs edits to src/features/builder/** transfer helpers, builderDraftStore, migrations, persistence, import/export codecs, or dependency files, stop with needs_scope_expansion.
Do not include DnD transfer parity, team CRUD/reorder, C7/C7a overview redesign, recommendations, nav promotion, or visual handoff polish in this slice.
Do not stage untracked docs/design files or smoke screenshots.

V1 team contracts are available as pure helpers: MAX_TEAMS, addTeam, renameTeam, deleteTeam, reorderTeams, isTeamEmpty, resetTeam, and applyTeamTemplate.
V1 team-management tests characterize add/max teams, empty delete direct, non-empty delete/reset confirmation, inline rename, and team helper behavior.
No V1 duplicate-team action exists; adding duplicate teams would be new behavior and is out of scope.
Tests should cover desktop/adaptive/mobile team overview visibility, active switching, add/max, rename commit/cancel/blur, delete/reset confirmation, template confirmation, and simple reorder.
risk: Functional C7 expands beyond original C7a read-only scope, but the user explicitly loosened the previous design blocker for a provisional functional teams surface.
Avoid dragging V1 page-shaped UI into V2; reuse helper contracts and store APIs through the V2 model facade.

tasks.title: Build Builder V2 functional team management overview
protected_files: src/features/builder/** except read-only imports from existing helpers/components
invariants: /builder behavior remains untouched.
/builder-v2 remains unpromoted from navigation.
Use existing team helper contracts; do not invent parallel team semantics.
Active team switching remains stable across desktop/adaptive/mobile.
Add respects MAX_TEAMS.
Rename trims names and ignores blank names.
Empty delete/reset may be direct; non-empty delete/reset requires confirmation before mutation.
Template application requires confirmation and preserves existing helper semantics.
Reorder uses simple accessible controls such as up/down buttons; no DnD.
The UI is explicitly provisional: dense functional list/management surface, not final cards/list/share design.
characterization_first: Red V2 test for add team, active switch, and max-team disabled state.
Red V2 test for rename confirm, Escape cancel, blur behavior, and blank-name no-op.
Red V2 test for empty delete direct and non-empty delete confirm/cancel.
Red V2 test for reset non-empty confirm/cancel.
Red V2 test for D-Tide 5 and D-Tide 10 template apply confirmation and resulting names/counts.
Red V2 test for reorder up/down preserving active team identity.
Red page tests that desktop, adaptive, and mobile expose the team management surface without breaking picker/import/export/transfer dialogs.
tasks.rollback: Delete/revert W8-local V2 teams overview/management edits and return to W7 behavior where V2 can switch active teams but cannot manage them.
scope_expansion_rules: If Worker needs edits to src/features/builder/** helper implementations, builderDraftStore, migrations, persistence, import/export codecs, or dependency files, stop with needs_scope_expansion.
Do not include final visual polish, list/cards product decision, screenshot/share layout, DnD reorder, recommendations, nav promotion, or C9 designer handoff bundle in W8.
Do not stage untracked docs/design files or smoke screenshots.
tasks.expected_output: Functional Builder V2 team management overview across desktop/adaptive/mobile, with add, switch, rename, reset/delete confirmations, D-Tide templates, simple accessible reorder, focused tests, and validation.

receipt.slice: C7a/C7 Builder V2 functional team management overview
behavior_added: Builder V2 now exposes a shared team management overview across desktop, adaptive, and mobile layouts.
Teams can be added up to MAX_TEAMS and the newly added team becomes active.
Teams can be switched from the management surface on every layout.
Inline rename supports commit, Escape cancel, blur commit, and blank-name no-op through builderDraftStore rename contracts.
Empty teams reset/delete directly; non-empty reset/delete open confirmation dialogs before mutation.
D-Tide 5 and D-Tide 10 templates open confirmation dialogs and then apply existing applyTeamTemplate semantics.
Teams can be reordered with accessible Move Up / Move Down controls while preserving active team identity.
Team summaries show slot occupants plus support, wheel, covenant, and posse signals for screenshot/readiness context.
behavior_preserved: /builder implementation files were not edited; V1 team helper and page-management tests remain green.
Builder V2 route/nav state remains unchanged and unpromoted from navigation.
builderDraftStore, persistence key/version, migrations, generated domain data, dependency files, import/export codecs, and protected helper implementations were not edited.
Existing Builder V2 import/export, transfer, quick-lineup, mobile drawer, adaptive picker, and assignment flows remained green after W8.
receipt.simplification: Centralized team-management UI in one BuilderV2TeamManagement component and kept team mutations behind the V2 model facade, reusing team-collection/builderDraftStore contracts instead of importing V1 page UI or inventing parallel semantics.
receipt.root_fix_patch_decision: Bounded root-fix inside Builder V2: add the missing team-management workflow to the V2 architecture rather than patching current BuilderPage or merging old branch UI.
receipt.dependency_review: No dependency changes made or needed.
characterization: Initial W8 red tests failed because model team operation APIs and the Builder V2 team management region did not exist.

Added model tests for add/max/activation, rename commit/cancel/blank/blur-ish behavior, empty delete direct, non-empty delete confirm/cancel, non-empty reset confirm/cancel, empty reset direct, D-Tide templates, and reorder preserving active team.
Added page tests for desktop summaries/switching, rename UI, destructive/template dialogs, and adaptive/mobile management exposure without opening picker flows.
Retained V1 team-collection and BuilderPage.team-management tests as helper/contract parity coverage.
validation: npx vitest run src/features/builder-v2/useBuilderV2Model.test.ts src/features/builder-v2/BuilderV2Page.test.tsx src/features/builder/team-collection.test.ts src/features/builder/BuilderPage.team-management.test.tsx (4 files, 80 tests passed after final empty-reset characterization)
npm run test:integration (7 files, 57 tests passed)
npm run lint (passed after final nested-control fix)
npm run build (passed after final nested-control fix; existing Vite chunk-size/plugin timing warnings only)

Browser smoke on http://127.0.0.1:5173/#/builder-v2: desktop team management region present, Add Team added one row, rename actions present; mobile management present, Add Team stayed on Mobile team overview and did not open a picker. Screenshot capture timed out on the asset-heavy page, so the final smoke used DOM/interaction checks only.

Controller addressed the untracked-file packaging issue, added explicit empty reset characterization, reran focused tests/lint/build/diff-check/browser smoke, and updated packet receipts. No product-code behavior findings remained from the review.

Browser screenshot capture timed out during W8 smoke because of the asset-heavy page; DOM/interaction smoke passed instead.
Drag-and-drop reorder and recommendation polish remain out of scope.

Optional DnD/recommendation polish in a later scoped slice if selected.
